steps.title: Find the contextual contrast
steps.content: Kamtekar's point: the lay view doesn't require character to be perfect — we only expect a helpful person to be frequently helpful. The blank contrasts with "frequently," so we need a word meaning something stronger than frequently — like "always" or "without exception."

steps.title: Match the word
steps.content:
"Unfailingly" means without ever failing — i.e., always — which is the stricter standard the lay view does not require.

(B) "self-servingly," (C) "grudgingly," and (D) "sporadically" don't fit the "more than frequently" contrast.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
